item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I don’t really have much of a complaint for this article, it does feel like a fever dream. A bit of backstory on…well, anything…would help the article I believe. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There was a brief period after I discovered this where I pimped it in IRC constantly. That phase has long since passed, but my love of this thing has not. Please, Recent Forum Posts lurkers, come check this out.

item.title: Idea!
item.content:encoded:
Maybe send him to law school?

Anyway, I like it. Have an upvote!

item.title: d u C K k k k k k k kk
item.content:encoded:
I LO V E IT 1 +++ +++ + ++ + + + +

Woahhh I needed to calm down man.

Anyway I think this was pretty enjoyable and it made me feel bad for the poor caveman.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So Midnighter is now an SCP?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know what, I think I've found the source of the duck plague. Patient Zero, ladies and gentlemen!

Someone out there should do a Tale about 1952 spreading a Duck virus around (either within the Foundation or prior to its containment).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Concurred.

I mean,

quack

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This should have been featured for today's tomfoolery. This is unrecognized greatness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Idunno, why can't there be more than one One True God?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But I thought this "God" is/are a bunch of fiction writers.

That said, this one takes the cake in terms of WTF to the point that you can't condemn it or applaud to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know what my answer for shit like this is, as far as headcanon is concerned? It's simple, really.


There really is a God in the Foundationverse. And He1 is absolutely batshit fucking crazy.

Footnotes
1. She? Xe? Idunno.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I cant judge this based on the usual scale for SCP's. I dont like it as an SCP but I think it works on its own as a stand alone idea.

It needs work on all the points discussed and more of an explanation of his new 'systems'. How does his new gut digest electronics and why in that manner? How does the helmet look beyond 'steel'? What is the purpose of the feathers and the feet?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As we have up votes and down votes we need up wuts and down wuts.

This was most certainly an up wut though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is such a bizarre idea that it really has no business being effective, but it's played so straight and so seriously that it somehow worked for me. Rather than just being wacky and confusing, it manages to become pretty creepy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The interpretive dancing actually seems more like tests of the new muscle attachments to me, but…

I dunno, I like it more than I don't like it. But it needs work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There needs to be a "wut" voting option. It was a good wut though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know… I rather like the sillyness, it's entertaining (very Far-Side-ish kind of fun). But coming not too long after reading the story of the cybernetic Wizard in his space suit and his posse of crime fighting cat women, this one feels a bit flat and pointless. Neutral vote for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Enh. This one's well written but just too weird for me. And it felt forced. It feels like you took it upon yourself to do one of those challenge articles with a really weird prompt consisting of: Caveman, Ducks, Electronics, and Interpretive dance. It just kind of… lurches from one point to the next. I'm impressed at how you sorta manage to tie it all together, but he end result is still just too scattered for me.

Also, I kept hoping for a glimpse of _why_ the 'star ducks' did what they did through the caveman's unreliable narration - but no, just a caveman kidnapped by duck aliens.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, I think the interview kills it for me. It's this bizarre mix of too plain, too wacky, and not what I'd expect a hyper-intelligent caveman to sound like. Otherwise, I appreciate the wackiness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I literally said "No." out loud after finishing reading and then proceeded to hand out my first ever downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And herons, don't forget herons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know, this pretty sums up my thoughts on this as well. It's incredibly creative and somewhat disturbing, but also stupid as fuuuuck. I don't know if I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And thus the SCP Turducken1 of Nonsense was born.

Footnotes
1. Turkey, geese, duck…Turduckeese?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Uhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhh…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the concept, and I like the zaniness of it, but some of the writing needs cleaned up, and it feels like it's missing something important.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Come on geese, you have to give them credit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As the author of the inter-dimensional turkey-eating Herons, I 100% approve this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's almost like people have to wing it when writing them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
GottaGoFeast, this is great. +1 you mad duckman you.

srsly tho i'm fucking stunned in a good way that you actually used my goofy name. I mean, it's just a lame play on "Locutus of Borg" and I'm just pleased as punch

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: These recent SCPs are just plain fowl.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…

Inter-dimensional turkey-eating Herons, a robot designed for the ethnic cleansing of geese, and now star-ducks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is fucking gibberish. +1

…

My only actual problem with this is the following sentence: "This helmet is responsible for its increased intelligence." That's speculation at this point. Now, you show plenty of EVIDENCE to make that the case, like where Gorb claims it is responsible for his intelligence, and where failing to feed him electronics makes him stupider, but its still speculation. I would either take it out or change to "This helmet is believed responsible for its increased intelligence."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I, uh. Hmm.

Like, it's far beyond how silly I like my mainlist articles. But it's so goddamn weird that it swings it back into being horrifying; I mean, ducks are cutting his legs off and barfing on the stumps, and making him eat electronics to maintain his intelligence. And then they stick giant duck feet on him and he starts doing forced interpretive dancing, and it's incredibly silly again.

I need some time to ponder how the hell I'm going to vote on this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Anatidaephobic ranting of a super-intelligent caveman. I love this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a caveman duck. That's pretty funny, but I have some issues.

+ show block
– hide block

In addition to the standard daily rations, SCP-1952 is to be fed 300g of electronic components every five days.

What counts as electronic components? This could use some examples given.

Should SCP-1952 become aggressive, it is to be sedated immediately by tranquilizer darts, which must be carried at all times by personnel.

How? Do all personnel on the site have guns? Also, make sure to spell-check.

It is approximately 30 years old and possesses dark skin along with black hair and eyes.

I thought it was wearing a helmet. Is it like a bicycle helmet where you can see its hair poking out underneath? This might be easier if you at least described the helmet beyond its material.

SCP-1952 also possesses webbed feet similar to that of Bucephala albeola (Bufflehead), several white feathers on its back, an additional digestive system and a mechanical helmet of unknown origin permanently implanted to its skull.

What's a Bufflehead? If it's a duck, put "Bufflehead duck" so the clarification is more clear. Also, I don't believe the correct term here is "implanted". Maybe "attached".

SCP-1952: …it was horrible, ok? What more do you want to know?

Ellipses shouldn't be put at the beginning of a sentence.

SCP-1952: NO! I won't talk anymore! I lost my family, my home, I was turned into a fucking monster, I can't get out of this place, I know nothing and nobody, no one cares about me, and you want me to talk MORE about it?

The capitalization of words looks really silly and unprofessional. Also, the swearing makes him sound like a whiny teenager.
